questions.stem: Your knowledge base contains a mix of detailed product manuals (long narrative content) and a short FAQ list. Which chunking strategy configuration would best serve both content types?

options.text: Use fixed-size chunking with very small chunks (50 tokens) to maximize precision for all content
Use semantic chunking for the narrative manuals and consider a separate index with fixed-size chunking for the structured FAQ
Use hierarchical chunking for everything since it automatically handles all content types optimally

questions.explanation: Semantic chunking splits on natural boundaries like paragraphs and topic shifts, producing coherent chunks ideal for narrative content like product manuals. Fixed-size chunking is better for structured data like FAQs. The guide notes that smaller chunks (100-200 tokens) give more precise matching for FAQ-style content, while larger chunks (500-1000 tokens) provide more context for narrative content.

questions.stem: The Agent Platform ingestion pipeline processes documents through six stages in order: Ingest, Extract text, Chunk, Enrich, ________, and Store.
questions.explanation: The six-stage ingestion pipeline is: (1) Ingest — receive and deduplicate content, (2) Extract text — pull plain text from source files, (3) Chunk — split text into searchable segments, (4) Enrich — add metadata like entity detection and summaries, (5) Embed — convert chunks into vector representations for semantic search, and (6) Store — save embedded chunks in the vector database.

questions.stem: An agent searches a knowledge base connected to SharePoint. User A can see a confidential HR document in SharePoint, but User B cannot. When both users ask the agent the same question, what happens?

options.text: Both users see the same results because the knowledge base indexes all documents uniformly
Only User A's results include chunks from the confidential document; User B's results are filtered based on their permissions
The agent detects the confidential document and refuses to answer either user

questions.explanation: The platform uses permission-aware search. Search results are filtered based on the requesting user's permissions. When a knowledge base is connected to a data source with access controls (like SharePoint), only documents the user has permission to see are included in results.

questions.stem: You want to track LLM cost breakdown by model and provider for a project over the past week. Which analytics API endpoint should you use?
questions.explanation: The Analytics API provides a dedicated GET /api/projects/:projectId/analytics/cost-breakdown endpoint that returns LLM cost breakdown by model and provider. It accepts 'from' and 'to' query parameters for time filtering. While the general metrics endpoint can also return sum_cost, the cost-breakdown endpoint is purpose-built for this use case.

questions.stem: A connector uses delta sync to keep a knowledge base updated from an external source. Which statement accurately describes how delta sync works?

options.text: Delta sync re-downloads and re-embeds all documents on every sync cycle to ensure consistency
Delta sync tracks what has changed since the last sync and only reprocesses modified or new documents; deleted documents are removed from the knowledge base
Delta sync only adds new documents but never updates or removes existing ones

questions.explanation: Connectors use delta sync to track changes since the last sync. Only modified or new documents are reprocessed through the ingestion pipeline, and deleted documents are removed from the knowledge base. This is much more efficient than full re-ingestion. A full sync can be triggered manually using mode: 'full' if needed.
